description: Aligned training data for vortex adapter. Format matches vortex_pair_facts.json exactly.

examples.context: Restricted 3-vortex: two strong vortices Γ₁=Γ₂=1, one weak test vortex Γ₃=ε≪1. Which geometric quantity is approximately conserved?
examples.truth: Q = r₁₂ + ε(r₁₃ + r₂₃) ≈ const (weighted perimeter)

examples.context: Restricted 3-vortex with Γ₃=0.01. What is the near-invariant quantity?
Restricted 3-vortex with Γ₃=0.02. What is the approximate conserved quantity?
Restricted 3-vortex with weak test vortex Γ₃=0.05. Near-invariant?
2D point-vortex system, N vortices at positions (xᵢ,yᵢ) with circulations Γᵢ, Kirchhoff equations. Which quantity is exactly conserved?
Point-vortex Hamiltonian. N vortices. What is exactly conserved?
2D point-vortex system with circulations Γᵢ at positions (xᵢ,yᵢ). Angular impulse (Kirchhoff 1876). Which quantity is exactly conserved?
Point-vortex angular impulse. What is conserved?
2D point-vortex system, Kirchhoff equations. Linear impulse components. Which is exactly conserved?
Point-vortex linear impulse x-component. Conserved quantity?
2D point-vortex system, Kirchhoff equations. Linear impulse y-component. Which is exactly conserved?
Point-vortex linear impulse y-component. What is conserved?
Two point vortices with equal circulations Γ₁=Γ₂. Kirchhoff equations. What is constant throughout the motion?
Equal-circulation vortex pair. What geometric quantity is conserved?
N point vortices with total circulation Γ_tot = Σ Γᵢ ≠ 0. Kirchhoff equations. What is conserved?
examples.truth: Xcm = Σᵢ Γᵢ xᵢ / Γ_tot = const,  Ycm = Σᵢ Γᵢ yᵢ / Γ_tot = const
distractors: Xcm drifts at velocity V = Γ_tot / (2π)

examples.context: Point-vortex centroid. Γ_tot ≠ 0. What is conserved?
examples.truth: Xcm = Σᵢ Γᵢ xᵢ / Γ_tot = const
distractors: Xcm = Σᵢ Γᵢ² xᵢ / Γ_tot = const

examples.context: Two point vortices with opposite circulations Γ₁=-Γ₂=Γ, separation d. Kirchhoff equations. Translational velocity of the pair?
Opposite-circulation vortex pair. Translation velocity?
Two equal co-rotating point vortices, each with circulation Γ, separated by distance d. Angular velocity of co-rotation?
Equal co-rotating vortex pair. Angular velocity?
Point-vortex equations in Hamiltonian form. The canonical coordinates are (qᵢ, pᵢ) = ?
Kirchhoff Hamiltonian structure. Canonical coordinates?
3-vortex point-vortex system. Is the system integrable (solvable in closed form)?
examples.truth: Yes: 3-vortex system is integrable (Aref 1979); conserved quantities H, Lz, Px, Py reduce it to quadratures
distractors: No: 3-vortex system is chaotic for generic initial conditions
Yes: only for equal circulations Γ₁=Γ₂=Γ₃

examples.context: Is the 3-vortex system integrable?
examples.truth: Yes: integrable (Aref 1979). H, Lz, Px, Py are conserved.

examples.context: 4-vortex point-vortex system. Integrability?
examples.truth: No: 4-vortex is generally non-integrable, can be chaotic

examples.context: Why is Q = r₁₂ + ε(r₁₃+r₂₃) a near-invariant in restricted 3-vortex?
examples.truth: The strong pair dominates. Weak vortex perturbs r₁₃, r₂₃ with amplitude ~ε, so weighting by ε cancels perturbation to leading order.
distractors: It's exactly conserved by Noether's theorem
All linear combinations of rᵢⱼ are conserved

examples.context: Restricted 3-vortex: what coefficient α minimizes variance of r₁₂ + α(r₁₃+r₂₃)?
Analogy: restricted 3-body has Jacobi integral. What is the vortex analog?
examples.truth: Q = r₁₂ + Γ₃(r₁₃+r₂₃). Weighted geometric invariant scaling test particle influence.

examples.context: Three symmetric vortices Γ₁=Γ₂=Γ₃ in equilateral triangle. Dynamics?
examples.truth: Relative equilibrium: triangle rotates rigidly, all rᵢⱼ exactly constant.

examples.context: Equilateral 3-vortex configuration. What happens?
examples.truth: Relative equilibrium: rigid rotation, rᵢⱼ = const exactly.
